---
title: "Stillingia tenuis"
slug: "stillingia-tenuis"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621847890/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Stillingia tenuis"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Malpighiales"
tax_family: "Euphorbiaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Malpighiales", "Euphorbiaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621847890/medium.jpg" alt="Stillingia tenuis" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jimw3">
jimw3</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341574426">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Malpighiales
-   **Family:** Euphorbiaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1530845)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** A rare species found in wet pine rocklands in southeastern and southwestern Florida.

## Natural history notes

A slender herbaceous plant characterized by its narrow leaves and small, inconspicuous flowers borne along upright stems. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as scrub, sandhills, and open pine flatwoods. It grows during the warmer months and is often a subtle component of groundcover vegetation in these ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/84300-Sambucus-canadensis)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3664)
